patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v4 5/5] mm: add tracepoints for zone lock

version_history.subject: [PATCH v2 4/4] mm: add tracepoints for zone lock
[PATCH v3 5/5] mm: add tracepoints for zone lock
[PATCH v4 5/5] mm: add tracepoints for zone lock

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds tracepoint instrumentation to zone lock acquire and release operations in the Linux kernel. The implementation uses a lightweight inline helper to check if tracing is enabled, and calls an out-of-line helper when tracing is active. When CONFIG_TRACING is disabled, the helpers compile to empty inline stubs, leaving the fast path unaffected. This patch introduces three new tracepoints: zone_lock_start_locking, zone_lock_acquire_returned, and zone_lock_released.

review_comments.author: Steven Rostedt
review_comments.summary:
reviewer noted that the zone lock tracepoints do not handle the case where CONFIG_TRACING is disabled, and suggested adding a check for this condition to avoid unnecessary function calls

The reviewer questioned the use of a boolean 'success' parameter in __zone_lock_trace_acquire_returned(), suggesting that it should always be set to true and proposing a simplified version.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Fri, 27 Feb 2026 16:00:27 +0000
Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

---

Why the "success" variable and not just:

	__zone_lock_trace_acquire_returned(zone, true);

 ?

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] cxl/acpi: fix CXL_ACPI=y + CXL_PMEM=m link failure

version_history.subject: [PATCH] cxl/acpi: fix CXL_ACPI=y + CXL_PMEM=m link failure

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes a link failure between the cxl_acpi and cxl_pmem modules when CXL_ACPI is built-in and CXL_PMEM is a module. The issue arises from a symbol dependency created by moving devm_cxl_add_nvdimm_bridge() to cxl_pmem.ko. The fix uses IS_REACHABLE() instead of IS_ENABLED() to prevent the link error.

review_comments.author: Harshit Mogalapalli
review_comments.summary: Approved the patch, stating that it fixes a link failure issue they encountered with their configuration. Also provided a Tested-by tag.
review_comments.raw_body:
Hi Gregory,

On 03/03/26 01:34, Gregory Price wrote:
> Commit e7e222ad73d9 ("cxl: Move devm_cxl_add_nvdimm_bridge() to
> cxl_pmem.ko") moved devm_cxl_add_nvdimm_bridge() from cxl_core into
> cxl_pmem, creating a symbol dependency from cxl_acpi to cxl_pmem.
> 
> When CXL_ACPI=y and CXL_PMEM=m, the built-in cxl_acpi attempts to
> link against a non-built-in cxl_pmem, which the linker cannot resolve.
> 
> CXL_PMEM depends on LIBNVDIMM, so LIBNVDIMM=m constrains CXL_PMEM to
> at most =m.
> 
> Change IS_ENABLED() to IS_REACHABLE(), which returns false when a
> built-in caller references a modular target, preventing the link error.
> 
> The result is if the pmem/nvdimm symbols are not reachable at build
> time, then at runtime it will always return -ENODEV.
> 
> Fixes: e7e222ad73d9 ("cxl: Move devm_cxl_add_nvdimm_bridge() to cxl_pmem.ko")
> Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>

We have run into the same issue as we have CXL_PMEM=m and CXL_ACPI=y, 
and this fix looks good to me.

Reviewed-by: Harshit Mogalapalli <harshit.m.mogalapalli@oracle.com>

also, gave this patch a go with our config:

Tested-by: Harshit Mogalapalli <harshit.m.mogalapalli@oracle.com>



Thanks,
Harshit

> ---
>   drivers/cxl/acpi.c | 2 +-
>   1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)
> 
> diff --git a/drivers/cxl/acpi.c b/drivers/cxl/acpi.c
> index 127537628817..7065413eda9f 100644
> --- a/drivers/cxl/acpi.c
> +++ b/drivers/cxl/acpi.c
> @@ -952,7 +952,7 @@ static int cxl_acpi_probe(struct platform_device *pdev)
>   	if (rc < 0)
>   		return rc;
>   
> -	if (IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_CXL_PMEM))
> +	if (IS_REACHABLE(CONFIG_CXL_PMEM))
>   		rc = device_for_each_child(&root_port->dev, root_port,
>   					   add_root_nvdimm_bridge);
>   	if (rc < 0)

review_comments.author: Jonathan Cameron
review_comments.summary: Asked about the usefulness of building CXL_PMEM as a module without this call being made, but did not provide any further feedback or concerns.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Mon,  2 Mar 2026 15:04:29 -0500
Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:

> Commit e7e222ad73d9 ("cxl: Move devm_cxl_add_nvdimm_bridge() to
> cxl_pmem.ko") moved devm_cxl_add_nvdimm_bridge() from cxl_core into
> cxl_pmem, creating a symbol dependency from cxl_acpi to cxl_pmem.
> 
> When CXL_ACPI=y and CXL_PMEM=m, the built-in cxl_acpi attempts to
> link against a non-built-in cxl_pmem, which the linker cannot resolve.
> 
> CXL_PMEM depends on LIBNVDIMM, so LIBNVDIMM=m constrains CXL_PMEM to
> at most =m.
> 
> Change IS_ENABLED() to IS_REACHABLE(), which returns false when a
> built-in caller references a modular target, preventing the link error.
> 
> The result is if the pmem/nvdimm symbols are not reachable at build
> time, then at runtime it will always return -ENODEV.
> 
> Fixes: e7e222ad73d9 ("cxl: Move devm_cxl_add_nvdimm_bridge() to cxl_pmem.ko")
> Signed-off-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>

What's the impact of this build combination wrt to whether the result is
actually useful?   Is there any point in building CXL_PMEM as a module
without this call being made?  I can't remember and normally don't care
much about PMEM :) 

> ---
>  drivers/cxl/acpi.c | 2 +-
>  1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)
> 
> diff --git a/drivers/cxl/acpi.c b/drivers/cxl/acpi.c
> index 127537628817..7065413eda9f 100644
> --- a/drivers/cxl/acpi.c
> +++ b/drivers/cxl/acpi.c
> @@ -952,7 +952,7 @@ static int cxl_acpi_probe(struct platform_device *pdev)
>  	if (rc < 0)
>  		return rc;
>  
> -	if (IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_CXL_PMEM))
> +	if (IS_REACHABLE(CONFIG_CXL_PMEM))
>  		rc = device_for_each_child(&root_port->dev, root_port,
>  					   add_root_nvdimm_bridge);
>  	if (rc < 0)

patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH] cxl/mbox: Use proper endpoint validity check upon sanitize

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v2 110/110] vfs: remove kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro

version_history.subject: [PATCH v2 110/110] vfs: remove kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch removes the kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro from the Linux kernel, replacing them with u64 type and format strings respectively. The changes are made throughout the tree, including in various file system-related files such as fs/dcache.c, fs/eventpoll.c, and include/linux/fs.h. The removal of these macros is necessary because i_ino has been widened to a 64-bit unsigned integer (u64), making the typedef and macro redundant.

review_comments.author: Jeff Layton (author)
review_comments.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the PRIino format macro being replaced with concrete format strings, specifically that the replacement for %PRIino in fs/inode.c was incorrect and should be %llx instead of %llu.

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch removes the kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro, but did not explicitly request any changes or express disagreement.
review_comments.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza
